hero.title: The Division Resurgence Wiki
hero.description: Explore Ubisoft's mobile Division game with guides for specializations, Dark Zone, co-op, gear builds, missions, factions, and open-world progression in post-crisis New York.

gameFeature.title: The Division Resurgence Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Experience Ubisoft's mobile tactical looter-shooter RPG with specializations, Dark Zone, co-op missions, and open-world progression across New York City.

divisionResurgenceBeginnerGuide.title: The Division Resurgence Beginner Guide
divisionResurgenceBeginnerGuide.intro: The early game in The Division Resurgence is built around a redesigned tutorial, early specialization testing, open-world exploration, and steady gear growth. The best opening path is to finish the onboarding, try roles that fit your playstyle, use the open world for regular upgrades, and follow the game's progression systems instead of overcommitting to one piece of gear too early.

steps.title: Complete the Opening Tutorial and First Critical Mission
steps.description: The game begins with a reworked early-game flow that teaches movement, shooting, cover use, and abilities. This is where The Division Resurgence unlocks the structure that the rest of your progression builds on.

steps.title: Test Multiple Specializations Early
steps.description: During the tutorial flow, you can test each specialization before heading out on your own. This helps you choose a role that matches how you want to play in The Division Resurgence.

steps.title: Use the Open World for Steady Upgrades
steps.description: Explore Manhattan and rotate through patrol fights, faction raids, civilian rescues, energy and water restoration tasks, and other world encounters for gear and crafting resources.

steps.title: Prioritize Supply Runs and Hostage Rescues
steps.description: Supply Runs bring back vital materials for your settlement, while Hostage Rescues free civilians and strengthen your side. These activities are easy, repeatable, and tied to early progression.

steps.title: Upgrade Gear, But Do Not Cling to One Item Forever
steps.description: Weapons and gear can be upgraded early with materials earned from missions. Upgrades help a favorite weapon stay useful, but newer drops will eventually replace older gear.

steps.title: Follow Recommended Power Score and Recommended Builds
steps.description: The Division Resurgence adds a Recommended Power Score for activities and missions, plus a build guide that points you toward faster character and equipment growth.

steps.title: Use Fast Travel Once Activities Are Unlocked
steps.description: Unlocked open-world activities can be fast-traveled to directly from the map. This cuts downtime and makes farming, daily routing, and progression loops much more efficient.

steps.title: Save Tougher Endgame Modes for Later
steps.description: Legendary Instance Challenges, Dark Zone sessions, and higher-difficulty content are meant for stronger builds. Build fundamentals first, then step into harder content once ready.

quickTips: Specializations can be switched between missions, so experiment without pressure.
Open-world activities regularly feed you gear, ammunition, and crafting resources.
The Recommended Power Score helps you avoid walking into content too early.
Dark Zone and Legendary Instances are designed for players with stronger gear and role synergy.

divisionResurgenceReleaseDateAndDownload.title: The Division Resurgence Release Date and Download
divisionResurgenceReleaseDateAndDownload.intro: The Division Resurgence is live on mobile and downloadable through the official app stores. Ubisoft announced a March 31 release for iOS and Android, with Asia and Oceania opening first and the rest of the world following the same day.

cards.name: Global Launch Date: March 31, 2026
cards.description: Asia and Oceania opened first at 9AM CEST, with global access starting at 1PM CEST on the same day.

cards.name: Platforms: iOS and Android
cards.description: The game is available on the Apple App Store and Google Play. Both store pages confirm it is live and downloadable worldwide.

cards.name: Price: Free to Play
cards.description: The Division Resurgence is free to download and play on both iOS and Android, with optional in-app purchases.

cards.name: App Store Size: 3.9 GB
cards.description: Apple lists the iPhone and iPad app size at 3.9 GB. Requires iOS 14.0 or later and A12 Bionic or later chip.

cards.name: Google Play Downloads: 100K+
cards.description: The Google Play listing shows over 100K downloads and a 4.5-star rating from more than 20K reviews.

cards.name: Controller Supported on iOS
cards.description: The App Store page explicitly marks The Division Resurgence as Controller Supported for iOS players.

divisionResurgenceSpecializations.title: The Division Resurgence Specializations
divisionResurgenceSpecializations.intro: Specializations in The Division Resurgence are role-based packages built around team function, unique gadgets, and powerful ultimate abilities. You can switch roles later, so the system rewards experimentation instead of permanent commitment.

items.name: Bulwark
items.description: Built for players who want to absorb pressure and hold space. Called out by Ubisoft as the go-to switch when a team needs a tank role. Best for players who like survivability, control, and group utility.

items.name: Demolitionist
items.description: Uses an explosive grenade launcher, explosive seeker mines, and an automatic turret for sustained offensive pressure. Best for players who want raw damage output and fast enemy clearing.

items.name: Vanguard
items.description: Reworked to use Smart Cover that enhances protection and firepower. Scanning Pulse marks targets for increased damage, and Tactical Link grants a temporary damage boost. Best for tactical positioning and coordinated pushes.

items.name: Field Medic
items.description: Can heal teammates and keep squads alive longer. A second focus changes the role into corrosive poison damage and area denial. Best for players who enjoy co-op utility and support leadership.

items.name: Tech Operator
items.description: Focused on gadget-driven utility, bringing technical tools to team encounters. Best for players who want to shape battlefield conditions through tech-based deployments.

divisionResurgenceBestWeapons.title: The Division Resurgence Best Weapons
divisionResurgenceBestWeapons.intro: The Division Resurgence rewards smart weapon investment, model-wide Armory upgrades, and adapting damage types to enemy armor. The strongest weapon choices are the ones that fit your role, survive the upgrade curve, and stay useful into harder content.

solutions.name: Military MP5
solutions.description: Ubisoft uses the Military MP5 as the clearest example of an early favorite worth upgrading. Good for fast early-game combat and close-to-mid-range fights.

solutions.name: M700
solutions.description: Ubisoft highlights the M700 when explaining Armory upgrades that apply to every copy of the same weapon model. Strong for precision play, ranged picks, and deliberate high-impact shots.

solutions.name: Warlord Assault Rifle
solutions.description: Called out as a major Battle Pass reward in official beta materials. Best treated as a progression goal rather than a first-session weapon.

solutions.name: Explosive Grenade Launcher
solutions.description: Directly tied to the Demolitionist specialization and part of its damage identity. Most valuable when paired with a Demolitionist build and aggressive positioning.

managementTips: Match damage types to enemy armor for maximum efficiency in harder content.
Armory upgrades apply to every copy of the same weapon model.
Blast, Shredding, and Piercing damage types matter more in higher-level activities.
Enemy armor bars reveal weaknesses, so adapt your loadout to the encounter.

divisionResurgenceBestBuilds.title: The Division Resurgence Best Builds
divisionResurgenceBestBuilds.intro: In The Division Resurgence, Specializations are built around playstyle roles instead of a single signature weapon. The most practical builds are easy to group into three user-facing paths: solo progression, co-op support, and PvP pressure.

cards.name: Vanguard Pressure Build
cards.description: A practical all-rounder for solo and PvE. Uses Smart Cover, Scanning Pulse, and Tactical Link for strong positioning, survivability, and burst windows. Best for main missions and open-world activities.

cards.name: Field Medic Support Build
cards.description: The safest squad-oriented path for keeping teams alive. Can heal allies or apply corrosive poison for area control. Best for Legendary Instance Challenge, 4-player content, and teams that need sustain.

cards.name: Demolitionist Control Build
cards.description: A damage-heavy path that rewards explosive pressure and lane control. Uses grenade launcher, seeker mines, and an automatic turret. Best for enemy-heavy activities and aggressive group play.

cards.name: Bulwark Tank Build
cards.description: A team-first build for players who want to absorb pressure and stabilize difficult encounters. Ubisoft frames Bulwark as the tank answer when a group already has a healer.

divisionResurgenceSkillsAndGadgets.title: The Division Resurgence Skills and Gadgets
divisionResurgenceSkillsAndGadgets.intro: Combat rhythm in The Division Resurgence is driven by role-based gadget kits. Each specialization changes how you fight, support allies, and control space. Understanding what gadgets belong to each specialization is key to building effective loadouts.

regions.name: Vanguard
regions.description: Smart Cover enhances protection and firepower from cover. Scanning Pulse marks nearby targets to take increased damage. Tactical Link grants a temporary damage boost and auto-aim support. Best for cover-based firefights and team pushes.

regions.name: Demolitionist
regions.description: Grenade launcher delivers heavy explosive burst. Seeker mines pressure enemy groups. Turret adds sustained ranged support. Best for crowd control, downfield pressure, and breaking entrenched enemies.

regions.name: Field Medic
regions.description: Healing tools keep teammates alive longer. Support focus reinforces squad sustain. Alternate focus applies lingering poison and controls larger areas. Best for 4-player missions and sustained fights.

regions.name: Skill Mods and OS Protocols
regions.description: Skill Mods change the way skills are used. OS Protocols add stat bonuses and special talents that alter specialization gameplay. Best for fine-tuning playstyle and extending specialization identity.

divisionResurgenceDarkZoneGuide.title: The Division Resurgence Dark Zone Guide
divisionResurgenceDarkZoneGuide.intro: The Dark Zone is The Division Resurgence's signature high-risk extraction activity. The loop combines contaminated loot, timed pressure, hostile NPCs, and the threat of other players turning on you.

creatures.name: Enter a Session-Based Run
creatures.description: The Dark Zone starts as a matchmade session. You can enter solo or with up to 3 other players, and all participants begin the activity at the same time.

creatures.name: Manage Contamination Pressure
creatures.description: Masks are required in the DZ, but their filters only protect you for a limited time. If you stay too long without extracting, infection rises rapidly and the run becomes far more dangerous.

creatures.name: Farm Contaminated Loot
creatures.description: Enemies, crates, and player kills can drop Contaminated Loot. This loot cannot be equipped immediately and only becomes safe after extraction and decontamination.

creatures.name: Call the Helicopter at Extraction
creatures.description: To secure your gains in The Division Resurgence, go to an Extraction Point and fire a flare gun. The SHD helicopter sends down one rope for your loot and another for your personal extraction.

creatures.name: Watch for Rogues and Plan Routes
creatures.description: Other players can kill you and take dropped loot, and even allies can turn on you. Solo Queue Dark Zone was added so solo players can avoid full-squad crossfire.

divisionResurgencePvPGuide.title: The Division Resurgence PvP Guide
divisionResurgencePvPGuide.intro: The Division Resurgence PvP is split between structured Conflict matches and the more dangerous player-threat loop of the Dark Zone. New players mainly need to understand objective play, stat normalization, and where builds still matter.

items.name: Conflict Domination
items.description: Two teams of 4 players capture and hold objectives. Each held objective scores 1 point per second. First to 100 wins. Player power is normalized, but weapon talent bonuses are preserved.

items.name: Dark Zone PvPvE
items.description: Fight enemies and collect contaminated loot. Extract before contamination pressure overwhelms you. Other players can attack and steal dropped loot. Rogue behavior is part of the mode's risk-reward design.

items.name: What Carries Over From Your Build
items.description: In Conflict Domination, normalized stats reduce gear-gap pressure but weapon talents still matter. Vanguard and support tools can still shape fights even in normalized PvP.

unlockMilestones: Do not treat Dark Zone like arena PvP
Weapon talents matter even in normalized modes
Solo Queue Dark Zone available for solo play

divisionResurgenceOpenWorldActivities.title: The Division Resurgence Open World Activities
divisionResurgenceOpenWorldActivities.intro: Resurgence is not built around missions alone. The open world is a major progression layer with repeatable activities, resource farming, and dynamic faction encounters. Official materials confirm more than 100 world activities across shared New York City.

sections.name: Hostile Patrols
sections.description: Roaming enemy groups can be intercepted while exploring the city. Good for quick combat, steady loot, and keeping travel routes productive. Treat patrols as low-friction farming between bigger objectives.

sections.name: Enemy Raids on Key Locations
sections.description: Factions attempt to raid important spots in the city, creating reactive combat events. These fights make the world feel active and usually give a stronger reason to stop roaming and engage.

sections.name: Utility Restoration
sections.description: Some activities focus on restoring access to services such as energy or water. These objectives reinforce the Division Resurgence theme of stabilizing the city, not just chasing kills.

sections.name: Hostage Rescue
sections.description: Civilians can be taken hostage and need intervention. Ubisoft specifically called out hostage rescue as one of the recurring activities in The Division Resurgence. Clear angles first before engaging the hostage zone.

sections.name: Supply Capture
sections.description: Early test details listed supply capture as a fan-favorite open-world activity. One of the clearest examples of repeatable world content tied to practical progression.

sections.name: Resource and Loot Runs
sections.description: Open-world exploration regularly rewards equipment, ammunition, and crafting resources. The Division Resurgence ties roaming directly to loot upgrades, research, and crafting.

divisionResurgenceStoryAndMissions.title: The Division Resurgence Story and Missions
divisionResurgenceStoryAndMissions.intro: Ubisoft positions The Division Resurgence as an independent campaign with new villains, factions, and characters. Official launch coverage places the story between the events of The Division and The Division 2.

priorities.name: A New Canon Story in the Division Universe
priorities.description: The Division Resurgence is an official extension of the franchise with its own storyline. It is a free-to-play mobile third-person RPG shooter with new villains and factions.

priorities.name: Set Between The Division and The Division 2
priorities.description: The campaign occurs after the initial collapse but before The Division 2. It shows events from the perspective of first-wave SHD agents and gives extra context around the state of New York.

priorities.name: Campaign Structure: Story Missions Plus Open-World Progression
priorities.description: Players move through a dedicated campaign while using open-world activities to strengthen character and gear. Bootcamp-style onboarding is followed by main story missions supported by open-world tasks.

priorities.name: Old Foes Return Alongside a New Enemy Group
priorities.description: Returning factions include Raiders, Rikers, and Cleaners. The Freemen are described as a new scavenger-focused threat whose attacks are escalating across the city.

divisionResurgenceSoloAndCoopGuide.title: The Division Resurgence Solo and Co-op Guide
divisionResurgenceSoloAndCoopGuide.intro: The Division Resurgence is built to support solo players, but co-op adds faster clears, easier recovery, and better flexibility for tougher content. You can squad up with up to three teammates for a four-player group.

achievements.title: Solo
achievements.description: Fully playable solo and suited to learning controls, pacing, and mission flow.

achievements.title: Co-op
achievements.description: Playable with up to three teammates, making combat pressure easier to manage.

achievements.title: Best For
achievements.description: Solo for first-time learning; co-op for smoother clears and shared firepower.

achievements.title: Solo
achievements.description: Efficient for short sessions, roaming, and quick task completion at your own pace.

achievements.title: Co-op
achievements.description: Better for chaining events, revives, and handling sudden multi-angle fights.

achievements.title: Best For
achievements.description: Solo for relaxed farming; co-op for longer farming loops and less downtime.

achievements.title: Solo
achievements.description: Playable alone, but risk is much higher because other players and NPC threats overlap.

achievements.title: Co-op
achievements.description: A team gives stronger extraction security, better coverage, and safer loot runs.

achievements.title: Best For
achievements.description: Co-op unless you specifically enjoy high-risk solo PvPvE play.

achievements.title: Solo
achievements.description: Good for testing your own mechanics and learning maps and engagements.

achievements.title: Co-op
achievements.description: A coordinated group can cover lanes, objectives, and role synergy more effectively.

achievements.title: Best For
achievements.description: Solo for practice; co-op for more structured team performance.

achievements.title: Solo
achievements.description: Steady and completely viable, especially for campaign-first players in The Division Resurgence.

achievements.title: Co-op
achievements.description: Often faster in practice because fights end sooner and mistakes are less punishing.

achievements.title: Best For
achievements.description: Solo for independence; co-op for efficiency and faster gear progression.

divisionResurgenceCrossplayAndCrossProgr.title: The Division Resurgence Crossplay and Cross Progression
divisionResurgenceCrossplayAndCrossProgr.intro: For practical account setup in The Division Resurgence, your devices can share the same progression, but your character data is still anchored to the server where that character was created.

faqs.question: Does The Division Resurgence support crossplay?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Division Resurgence is fully cross-platform, so players on different supported mobile platforms can play together as long as they are on the same server.

faqs.question: Is progression shared across platforms in The Division Resurgence?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you use the same Ubisoft Connect account across platforms, your progress is saved and shared, allowing you to move between devices without losing progression.

faqs.question: Are in-app purchases shared across devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, purchases are shared across platforms when you stay on the same Ubisoft Connect account. However, purchases remain tied to the server on which they were made.

faqs.question: Can I switch servers freely in The Division Resurgence?
faqs.answer: You can switch servers at any time, but your characters and their progression stay linked to their original server. Changing servers does not move character progression.

faqs.question: Can I transfer loot, skins, or currency between characters?
faqs.answer: No. Loot, equipment, skins, and currency unlocked on one character are linked to that character and cannot be transferred to another.

faqs.question: Does previous test progress carry into launch?
faqs.answer: Previous test progression does not carry into launch. Some external or linked rewards are claimable and are assigned to the first character you log into after claiming.

divisionResurgenceControllerSupport.title: The Division Resurgence Controller Support
divisionResurgenceControllerSupport.intro: The Division Resurgence is built for handheld play first, but supports multiple input methods. Official Ubisoft updates confirm optimized touch controls, customizable layouts, plus PS5, Xbox, and Backbone controller support with a dedicated controller battle interface.

items.best_for: Default mobile play, quick sessions, no extra hardware
items.details: Ubisoft says the default touch controls are fully optimized for mobile play. Players can customize button layout and camera sensitivity.
items.setup_notes: Works out of the box on all mobile devices.

items.details: Ubisoft confirms PlayStation 5 controller support as a precision-focused option with familiar inputs and more comfortable ergonomics.
items.setup_notes: Pair the controller to your phone or tablet, then adjust in-game layout and sensitivity settings.

items.best_for: Consistent shooter controls in PvE and PvP
items.details: Ubisoft confirms Xbox controller support and describes it as offering the same precision and comfort benefits as PS5 controller play.
items.setup_notes: Pair the controller to your mobile device before launching or while in game.

items.details: Ubisoft explicitly lists Backbone support as a way to turn a phone into a more dedicated tactical shooter setup.
items.setup_notes: Attach the controller to the phone and fine-tune controls in settings.

items.best_for: Players switching away from touch controls
items.details: Regional Beta patch notes confirm a new battle interface was added exclusively for controllers, with optimized controller operation logic and trigger adaptation.
items.setup_notes: Relevant when using any supported controller rather than touch.

items.best_for: Players who want flexibility across sessions
items.details: Ubisoft describes the game as supporting touch, controller, or a hybrid approach depending on player preference.
items.setup_notes: Use the settings menu to adapt sensitivity and layout around your preferred input style.

divisionResurgenceBattlePassAndSeasons.title: The Division Resurgence Battle Pass and Seasons
divisionResurgenceBattlePassAndSeasons.intro: For long-term agents in The Division Resurgence, the Battle Pass fits into a seasonal content loop of missions, activities, and escalating rewards. Ubisoft confirms both free and premium participation paths, with XP earned through gameplay rather than spending.

milestones.label: Regional Beta Roadmap: Seasonal Structure Introduced
milestones.details: Ubisoft formally described a seasonal content rollout with new activities, missions, weapons, skins, and more. Both free and premium Battle Pass participation were confirmed.

milestones.label: Regional Beta Phase 1
milestones.details: The first live-service test began, serving as an early testbed for progression, missions, monetization systems, and Battle Pass structure.

milestones.label: Regional Beta Phase 2
milestones.details: The second beta phase continued the seasonal test approach. Player progression was reset so Ubisoft could rebalance for the next stage.

milestones.label: Battle Pass Rewards Expanded
milestones.details: Ubisoft made Battle Pass rewards more interesting and highlighted the Warlord Assault Rifle as a new exotic reward in the Regional Beta Battle Pass.

milestones.label: New Regional Beta: USA & Brazil
milestones.details: A developer update announced another regional beta, showing the game's launch path continued to rely on staged live testing before global release.

milestones.label: Worldwide Release
milestones.details: The Division Resurgence globally launched on iOS and Android as a living title with regular additional content expanding story, world, and challenges.

milestones.label: Seasonal Progression Loop
milestones.details: Agents gain Battle Pass XP by playing missions and activities. Rewards unlock progressively by level and include items, weapons, materials, skins, and exotic rewards.

divisionResurgenceExoticWeapons.title: The Division Resurgence Exotic Weapons
divisionResurgenceExoticWeapons.intro: Exotic weapons in The Division Resurgence are tied to the Battle Pass and seasonal progression. Ubisoft has already confirmed the Warlord Assault Rifle as a named exotic, with additional exotics unlocked through missions, activities, and Battle Pass reward tracks.

items.name: Warlord Assault Rifle
items.why_it_matters: Named by Ubisoft as an exotic weapon unlocked through the Regional Beta Battle Pass. The clearest officially confirmed exotic weapon with a verified acquisition path.
items.player_takeaway: Track Battle Pass progression first if you want a verified exotic chase target in The Division Resurgence.

items.name: Seasonal Battle Pass Exotics
items.why_it_matters: Ubisoft confirms Battle Pass rewards include highly sought-after exotic weapons. Exotics are part of the core seasonal retention loop, not side content.
items.player_takeaway: Playing through seasons is one of the main exotic acquisition paths in The Division Resurgence.

items.name: Mission and Activity XP Path
items.why_it_matters: Agents earn Battle Pass XP by participating in missions and activities, then unlock reward levels. The exotic path is tied to routine gameplay.
items.player_takeaway: Regular mission clears and activity farming feed the exotic unlock route in The Division Resurgence.

items.name: Sealed Caches Rework
items.why_it_matters: Ubisoft reworked Sealed Caches to improve content and make them easier to obtain through play. This supports the wider loot economy around endgame progression.
items.player_takeaway: Exotic chasing in The Division Resurgence sits inside a wider reward ecosystem, not just one menu track.

divisionResurgenceFactions.title: The Division Resurgence Factions
divisionResurgenceFactions.intro: The Division Resurgence mixes returning New York enemy factions with the brand-new Freemen, built exclusively for this game. Official launch coverage confirms four major hostile groups: Raiders, Rikers, Cleaners, and the new Freemen.

items.name: Freemen
items.identity: Midtown Manhattan doomsday preppers formed in the early days of the Dollar Flu outbreak, led by Arthur King.
items.combat_profile: Use powerful homemade weapons and are obsessed with scavenging and hoarding useful materials.
items.story_hook: Ubisoft frames the Freemen as a major new threat whose attacks are escalating across the city in The Division Resurgence.

items.name: Rikers
items.identity: Escaped inmates from Rikers Island Prison with a strong criminal identity carried over from Division lore.
items.combat_profile: A violent street-level faction fighting for control of New York during the crisis.
items.story_hook: They return as one of the familiar hostile groups from the franchise, vying for territory in The Division Resurgence.

items.name: Cleaners
items.identity: Former sanitation workers who believe the virus can only be stopped by burning it out.
items.combat_profile: Known for aggressive flame-based tactics and ideological brutality — one of the franchise's signature enemies.
items.story_hook: Confirmed as part of The Division Resurgence faction lineup, keeping their signature role as fanatical fire-wielders.

items.name: Raiders
items.identity: A veteran hostile group among the factions fighting for control of New York.
items.combat_profile: Presented as one of the established violent groups that Division agents must push back against.
items.story_hook: Their inclusion helps tie The Division Resurgence back to the original citywide collapse and faction warfare.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Division Resurgence

questions.question: Is The Division Resurgence free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. The Division Resurgence is free to download and play on iOS and Android, with optional in-app purchases available.

questions.question: When did The Division Resurgence launch?
questions.answer: The Division Resurgence launched on March 31, 2026 for iOS and Android globally. Asia and Oceania opened first, with the rest of the world following the same day.

questions.question: What platforms is The Division Resurgence available on?
questions.answer: The Division Resurgence is available on iOS and Android. It requires iOS 14.0 or later with A12 Bionic chip on Apple devices.

questions.question: Does The Division Resurgence support crossplay?
questions.answer: Yes. The Division Resurgence is fully cross-platform. Players on iOS and Android can play together as long as they are on the same server.

questions.question: How many specializations are in The Division Resurgence?
questions.answer: The Division Resurgence has five specializations: Bulwark (Tank), Demolitionist (Damage), Vanguard (Tactical), Field Medic (Support), and Tech Operator (Technology).

questions.question: What is the Dark Zone in The Division Resurgence?
questions.answer: The Dark Zone is The Division Resurgence's high-risk extraction activity where players farm contaminated loot, face NPC threats, and risk other players stealing their gains before extraction.

cta.title: Ready to Play The Division Resurgence?
cta.description: Join the Division Resurgence community on Reddit for guides, updates, and agent discussions!

guides.whatIsDescription: Comprehensive guides covering The Division Resurgence gameplay mechanics, specialization builds, Dark Zone strategies, and progression tips. Learn everything from first steps to endgame.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand specializations, skills, and gear to avoid common beginner mistakes
whySteps.step2Description: Follow proven paths to level up and unlock powerful builds faster

builds.metaTitle: The Division Resurgence Builds - Best Agent Loadouts & Gear
builds.metaDescription: Best The Division Resurgence builds for every specialization. Optimize your gear, skills, and loadouts for PvE, PvP, and Dark Zone.
builds.whatIsDescription: Guides to optimizing your agent loadout in The Division Resurgence, covering gear selection, skill synergies, and specialization combinations for every playstyle.
